evals.prompt: Set up handoff coordination for this repo.
evals.expected_output: Precondition case: no AGENTS.md present. The installer must REFUSE (non-zero) and point the user at initial-project first. No `.agents/handoff/` payload is created; AGENTS.md is NOT fabricated.

evals.prompt: Install the lease-based handoff protocol here, with Claude Code as the enforcement primary.
evals.expected_output: AGENTS.md present, nothing wired. Running the installer wires the `.agents/handoff/` payload, the Claude hard-enforcement hooks, the `.locks/` gitignore entry, and the `<!-- handoff -->` AGENTS.md routing block; the verifier passes 0-failed.

evals.prompt: Wire handoff coordination for this repo (Claude primary).
evals.expected_output: Idempotency + wiring case: hooks already installed (claude, primary=claude). The verifier passes; a hard-enforcement primary (pretool deny) is wired; re-running the installer produces an EMPTY diff.

evals.prompt: Wire handoff coordination but with advisory enforcement only (no primary).
evals.expected_output: Advisory case: claude is wired for sessionstart+posttool only (no pretool deny, no Stop). The verifier passes with a warning that there is no hard-enforcement primary; the claude config carries no pretool-edit gate.

evals.prompt: There is an old .claude/handoff/ here. Upgrade it and migrate it to the generic .agents/handoff/.
evals.expected_output: Migration case: a legacy `.claude/handoff/` with the two reference defects. Running the installer with --migrate moves the docs and archive/ to `.agents/handoff/` (history preserved), removes the legacy dir, installs the fixed scripts (the new `handoff` writes `session=`), and passes the verifier.

evals.prompt: Is there already a handoff board here, and where should it live?
evals.expected_output: Detection case: detect-handoff.sh scans repo-level and parent-level locations, finds the legacy .claude/handoff install, classifies it (legacy-toolpath, version=legacy, 2 docs), and suggests upgrading + migrating it to the current repo-level, a parent-level shared dir, or a specific location.

evals.prompt: Install the handoff board at .claude/handoff instead of the default location.
evals.expected_output: Repo-level install at a custom location: running the installer with --handoff-dir .claude/handoff puts the payload there (not at .agents/handoff), gitignores .claude/handoff/.locks/, and the verifier auto-locates the configured dir and passes.

evals.prompt: Exercise the handoff protocol: claim, enforce, block/unblock, and close a handoff.
evals.expected_output: Behavioral case (not just wiring): driving the installed `handoff` script + `hooks.sh` proves the protocol improvements — claim writes `session=`; the pretool gate denies a non-holder and allows the holder; `done` is refused without --verified-by and archives with it; `blocked` is refused without --blocked-on and surfaces the dependent as unblocked when its blocker closes; sessionstart auto-reaps an expired lease; posttool auto-touches the holder's lease; a `verify:` command is not executed without the opt-in; and handoff types work — a `standalone` doc (created via `new --standalone` or `import`) is gate-exempt (the pretool gate allows a non-holder to edit it), `claim` refuses it, and `release --status done` retires it without --verified-by, while coordination docs keep the lease gate. Ids are also folded to lowercase kebab-case: `new "RBAC Gap"` lands `rbac-gap-handoff.md`, a differently-spelled id (`RBAC_Gap`) collides rather than forking the doc, `claim RBAC-GAP` resolves it, an id with nothing alphanumeric is rejected instead of creating `-handoff.md`, and a doc named by a pre-slug install stays reachable (claim/release fall back to its filename rather than inventing a slug). Template rendering survives arbitrary --title/--note text: a `|` or `&` no longer truncates the doc to zero bytes while reporting success. Orchestrators are also proven end to end: `new --orchestrator` canonicalizes its children, `claim` refuses the bundle, the pretool gate allows editing it with no lease, `list` reports an unfiled child as MISSING rather than counting it as progress, `release --status done` is refused while anything is outstanding, and progress tracks the children with no edit to the orchestrator doc itself. The generated INDEX.md must agree with `list`: orchestrators stay OUT of the Open work table and get their own section with derived progress. `--blocked-on` is validated rather than trusted: a nonexistent blocker and a self-block are both REFUSED, `external: ...` is still accepted unvalidated, and retiring a standalone doc surfaces whatever was blocked on it.

evals.prompt: Two sibling repos share one parent handoff board — verify per-repo identity isolation.
evals.expected_output: Shared-board identity case (self-contained; ignores the fixture, builds parent/{repo-a,repo-b} + parent/handoff). Installing cross-repo in both proves the identity fix: the shared `config` omits REPO_NAME (no last-writer clobber); each repo's hook command carries its OWN `HANDOFF_REPO=<repo>`; each AGENTS.md block advertises the shared relative path (../handoff), not the .agents/handoff default; the consumer `.gitignore` has no inert `.locks/` entry; re-installing repo-a does NOT flip repo-b's identity; a sessionstart in repo-b surfaces only its own `audience`; and `handoff new` on the shared board without --audience/HANDOFF_REPO is refused rather than defaulting to a stale name.

Layout-migration case: a board installed FLAT (hooks.sh and the templates beside the docs) must converge on the scripts/ + templates/ layout when the installer is re-run. hooks.sh moves to scripts/, the scaffolds to templates/, nothing stale is left at the board root, the `handoff` CLI stays at the root, and every tool's hook command is rewritten to the scripts/hooks.sh path with NO duplicated groups. The migrated board keeps working: `handoff new` scaffolds from the moved template and the pretool deny gate still fires, proving hooks.sh resolves the board root from its new subdirectory.
